$comment: Applicable reporting granularities per GEFEG BatteryPass-Ready attribute, keyed by SAMM group then GEFEG attribute name. Each value is an ORDERED list of oec:DPPGranularity levels: the FIRST entry is the primary/canonical level at which the attribute is authoritatively established (this drives which GS1 resolver resource serves it and the item-level EPCIS fold in scripts/reconstruct-passport-from-epcis.ts, which keys on primary == ItemLevel); the REMAINING entries are further levels at which the attribute may legitimately appear. GRANULARITY IS THE PRODUCT HIERARCHY ONLY — ModelLevel, BatchLevel, ItemLevel — matching the GS1 Digital Link AIs (/01, /01+10/{lot}, /01+21/{serial}). 'Per manufacturing site' is NOT a granularity: it mixes source/location with granularity. The model has a single source of truth (ModelLevel); the same model built at different sites is still ModelLevel, and the site is a source dimension carried by EPCIS events via readPoint / bizLocation. Principle: data lives at the level where it is authoritatively established and a finer passport RESOLVES it up the GS1 Digital Link hierarchy rather than restating it — so model master-data that exists before anything is produced or transformed (product/model identity, rated design specs, chemistry, category, mass, warranty terms, conformity documents, symbols, dismantling/safety/spare-part information, due diligence, manufacturer information) is ModelLevel ONLY; production facts that genuinely vary per run are BatchLevel (manufacturing date + place + facility, carbon footprint and its lifecycle breakdown + performance class + label, recycled-content and renewable shares), with material composition / hazardous substances / critical raw materials at ModelLevel and BatchLevel when sourcing varies; in-use measured values (state of charge, remaining capacity, fades, cycles, throughput, time in extreme temperatures, deep-discharge count) and the physical battery status exist only at ItemLevel. ONLY the passport-INSTANCE metadata spans levels, because a model, batch and item passport each has its own: DPPStatus (a GTIN/model or a production batch can be deactivated/archived, as can an item), DPPGranularity (each instance declares its own level) and Date-timeOfLatestUpdateOfDPP. Levels: ModelLevel, BatchLevel, ItemLevel. Model / Batch attributes are served by the GS1 resolver master-data resource (01 / 01+10 Digital Links); Item attributes are reconstructed by folding the EPCIS event stream for the serialized battery (01+21). SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: these levels are authored as oec:reportingGranularity on the eubat:/oec: terms in the ontology and this file is generated from them. Machine-readable form of the level split in docs/EPCIS_AND_BATTERYPASS_READY.md; coverage asserted by scripts/test-batterypass-conformance.ts.
